intro: The hip is a Ball-and-Socket joint. It allows for the widest range of movement.
notes: Consists of a rounded head (ball) fitting into a cup (socket).

hotspots.label: Acetabulum (Socket)
hotspots.description: The Acetabulum is the deep cup-shaped cavity in the pelvic bone where the leg attaches.
hotspots.examTip: It is much deeper than the shoulder socket, providing more stability but less movement.

hotspots.label: Femur Head (Ball)
hotspots.description: The rounded top of the thigh bone. It is covered in very smooth cartilage to spin freely inside the socket.
hotspots.examTip: The fit is so perfect that suction helps hold it in place.

hotspots.label: Rotational Movement
hotspots.description: This structure allows the leg to swing forward, backward, sideways, and rotate.
hotspots.examTip: This joint type allows for the greatest range of motion in the body.
